title: The Kimi K3 Moment
excerpt: I’ve been running Kimi K3 alongside Claude on my normal coding work, and for all practical purposes I can’t tell them apart. Same tasks, same quality of outp...
content_html:
I’ve been running Kimi K3 alongside Claude on my normal coding work, and for all practical purposes I can’t tell them apart. Same tasks, same quality of output, and near identical token counts to get there. I expected an open model to be sloppier or to grind through more tokens on the way to the same answer, and neither turned out to be true.

The prices are nowhere near each other. K3’s API runs $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output. Claude’s top model costs $10 and $50 for the same units. The subscription side is even more lopsided. Kimi’s paid plans start at $19 a month, and the $39 coding tier is far more generous than anything Claude sells anywhere near that price. Claude’s plans are metered tightly enough that a normal day of agent work can chew through the allowance before lunch.

Then there’s the fine print. Claude couldn’t sustain Fable access on the twenty dollar plan, so they turned it off, and the plan quietly falls back to Opus. When the headline model on your plan can be switched off because the economics don’t work, the plan was never really selling you the headline model. Kimi’s tiers don’t come with that asterisk.

Step back and the bigger story is what an unmitigated failure US AI policy has been. The administration held Fable back, and what finally shipped is a hindered version that refuses whole categories of work. Meanwhile a frontier quality model with none of those restrictions is a download away, released by a Chinese lab the US government has no ability to regulate. Whatever the theory behind gating American models was, it plainly wasn’t thought through, because the only people the gates constrain are American customers. Semgrep found GLM 5.2 beating Claude on their cyber benchmarks for exactly this reason. The restricted model declines the work and the open one just does it.

And it isn’t only Kimi. GLM 5.2 came out under an MIT license, beats the latest Opus release on real work while not even claiming to be frontier, and costs a fraction of it. OpenAI got pushed through the same government gauntlet with GPT-5.6 but came out the other side able to put their flagship on the twenty dollar plan. Whatever you think of OpenAI, they have runway here that Anthropic clearly doesn’t.

I think I can see where this goes. The government will try to regulate AI and open source in particular, and it will run the playbook it ran for the auto industry. Decades of subsidies, bailouts, and protective tariffs produced American carmakers that sell trucks at home and barely register anywhere else in the world. I expect the current administration to reach for the same tools here. Public private partnerships propping up domestic models that only get used inside the US and can’t compete internationally. That’s a sad future where America is the one country without access to the best models at the best prices, buying models deeply tied to the corrupt Trump administration that are neither the highest quality nor the cheapest. Until then, at least, I can’t come up with a reason to keep paying for Claude.

sentences.zh: 克劳德的计划是严格计量的，正常一天的特工工作可以在午餐前用完津贴。

sentences.en: Step back and the bigger story is what an unmitigated failure US AI policy has been.
sentences.zh: 退一步来说，更大的故事是美国人工智能政策的彻底失败。

sentences.en: The administration held Fable back, and what finally shipped is a hindered version that refuses whole categories of work.
sentences.zh: 政府阻止了《神鬼寓言》，最终发布的是一个拒绝所有类别作品的受阻版本。

sentences.en: Meanwhile a frontier quality model with none of those restrictions is a download away, released by a Chinese lab the US government has no ability to regulate.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，可以下载不受这些限制的前沿质量模型，该模型是由美国政府无能力监管的中国实验室发布的。

sentences.en: Whatever the theory behind gating American models was, it plainly wasn’t thought through, because the only people the gates constrain are American customers.
sentences.zh: 无论限制美国模式背后的理论是什么，它显然都没有经过深思熟虑，因为大门限制的唯一人是美国消费者。

sentences.en: Semgrep found GLM 5.2 beating Claude on their cyber benchmarks for exactly this reason.
sentences.zh: Semgrep 发现 GLM 5.2 在其网络基准测试中击败了 Claude，正是因为这个原因。

sentences.en: The restricted model declines the work and the open one just does it.
sentences.zh: 受限模型会拒绝这项工作，而开放模型只会完成这项工作。

sentences.en: And it isn’t only Kimi.
sentences.zh: 不仅仅是基米。

sentences.en: GLM 5.2 came out under an MIT license, beats the latest Opus release on real work while not even claiming to be frontier, and costs a fraction of it.
sentences.zh: GLM 5.2 在 MIT 许可下发布，在实际工作中击败了最新的 Opus 版本，但甚至没有声称自己是前沿的，而且成本只是它的一小部分。

sentences.en: OpenAI got pushed through the same government gauntlet with GPT-5.6 but came out the other side able to put their flagship on the twenty dollar plan.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 与 GPT-5.6 一起经历了同样的政府挑战，但最终却能够将他们的旗舰产品纳入 20 美元计划。

sentences.en: Whatever you think of OpenAI, they have runway here that Anthropic clearly doesn’t.
sentences.zh: 无论你如何看待 OpenAI，他们在这里拥有 Anthropic 显然没有的跑道。

blocks.html_en: I think I can see where this goes. The government will try to regulate AI and open source in particular, and it will run the playbook it ran for the auto industry. Decades of subsidies, bailouts, and protective tariffs produced American carmakers that sell trucks at home and barely register anywhere else in the world. I expect the current administration to reach for the same tools here. Public private partnerships propping up domestic models that only get used inside the US and can’t compete internationally. That’s a sad future where America is the one country without access to the best models at the best prices, buying models deeply tied to the corrupt Trump administration that are neither the highest quality nor the cheapest. Until then, at least, I can’t come up with a reason to keep paying for Claude.

sentences.en: I think I can see where this goes.
sentences.zh: 我想我能明白这会发生什么。

sentences.en: The government will try to regulate AI and open source in particular, and it will run the playbook it ran for the auto industry.
sentences.zh: 政府将尝试监管人工智能，特别是开源，并将按照汽车行业的剧本运行。

sentences.en: Decades of subsidies, bailouts, and protective tariffs produced American carmakers that sell trucks at home and barely register anywhere else in the world.
sentences.zh: 数十年的补贴、救助和保护性关税使得美国汽车制造商在国内销售卡车，几乎在世界其他地方注册。

sentences.en: I expect the current administration to reach for the same tools here.
sentences.zh: 我希望现任政府能够在这里使用同样的工具。

sentences.en: Public private partnerships propping up domestic models that only get used inside the US and can’t compete internationally.
sentences.zh: 公私合作伙伴关系支撑的国内模式仅在美国境内使用，无法在国际上竞争。

sentences.en: That’s a sad future where America is the one country without access to the best models at the best prices, buying models deeply tied to the corrupt Trump administration that are neither the highest quality nor the cheapest.
sentences.zh: 这是一个悲惨的未来，美国是一个无法以最优惠的价格获得最好模型的国家，购买的模型与腐败的特朗普政府密切相关，这些模型既不是最高质量也不是最便宜的。

sentences.en: Until then, at least, I can’t come up with a reason to keep paying for Claude.
sentences.zh: 至少在那之前，我想不出继续为克劳德付钱的理由。

content_html_zh:
在我的正常编码工作中，我一直在与 Claude 一起运行 Kimi K3，但出于所有实际目的，我无法区分它们。 相同的任务、相同的输出质量以及几乎相同的令牌数量来实现这一目标。 我预计开放模型会更加草率，或者在得到相同答案的过程中需要磨练更多的标记，但结果都不是真的。

价格相差甚远。 K3 的 API 运行每百万输入代币 3 美元，每百万输出代币 15 美元。 相同型号的 Claude 顶级型号售价分别为 10 美元和 50 美元。 订阅方面更是一边倒。 Kimi 的付费套餐起价为每月 19 美元，而 39 美元的编码套餐比 Claude 销售的任何接近该价格的产品都要慷慨得多。 克劳德的计划是严格计量的，正常一天的特工工作可以在午餐前用完津贴。

然后是精美的印刷品。 克劳德无法维持 20 美元计划的《神鬼寓言》访问权限，因此他们将其关闭，该计划悄悄地退回到 Opus。 当你的计划中的标题模型因为经济学不起作用而可以关闭时，该计划从来没有真正向你推销标题模型。 Kimi 的等级没有那个星号。

退一步来说，更大的故事是美国人工智能政策的彻底失败。 政府阻止了《神鬼寓言》，最终发布的是一个拒绝所有类别作品的受阻版本。 与此同时，可以下载不受这些限制的前沿质量模型，该模型是由美国政府无能力监管的中国实验室发布的。 无论限制美国模式背后的理论是什么，它显然都没有经过深思熟虑，因为大门限制的唯一人是美国消费者。 Semgrep 发现 GLM 5.2 在其网络基准测试中击败了 Claude，正是因为这个原因。 受限模型会拒绝这项工作，而开放模型只会完成这项工作。

不仅仅是基米。 GLM 5.2 在 MIT 许可下发布，在实际工作中击败了最新的 Opus 版本，但甚至没有声称自己是前沿的，而且成本只是它的一小部分。 OpenAI 与 GPT-5.6 一起经历了同样的政府挑战，但最终却能够将他们的旗舰产品纳入 20 美元计划。 无论你如何看待 OpenAI，他们在这里拥有 Anthropic 显然没有的跑道。

我想我能明白这会发生什么。 政府将尝试监管人工智能，特别是开源，并将按照汽车行业的剧本运行。 数十年的补贴、救助和保护性关税使得美国汽车制造商在国内销售卡车，几乎在世界其他地方注册。 我希望现任政府能够在这里使用同样的工具。 公私合作伙伴关系支撑的国内模式仅在美国境内使用，无法在国际上竞争。 这是一个悲惨的未来，美国是一个无法以最优惠的价格获得最好模型的国家，购买的模型与腐败的特朗普政府密切相关，这些模型既不是最高质量也不是最便宜的。 至少在那之前，我想不出继续为克劳德付钱的理由。
